name: LightRAG
pricing_notes: Fully open source; pay only for LLM API calls used during indexing/retrieval

auth.notes: Self-hosted; configure your LLM provider API key in settings

performance.notes: Indexing is slow (knowledge graph extraction via LLM); queries 1-5s depending on mode; faster than naive RAG for complex multi-hop questions

agent_readiness.notes: Graph-enhanced RAG that builds knowledge graphs from documents alongside vector embeddings. Supports multiple retrieval modes: naive, local (entity-centric), global (community-level), hybrid. Significantly outperforms naive RAG on multi-hop reasoning questions. Research-originated (HKUDS lab). Integrates with Neo4j, NetworkX, and various vector stores.

gotchas: Indexing is expensive — extracts entities/relationships via LLM calls (slow + costly)
Research-grade software; production readiness is limited
Documentation is academic/sparse — fewer guides than LangChain/LlamaIndex
Knowledge graph extraction quality depends heavily on document structure
best_when: Knowledge-intensive RAG applications where multi-hop reasoning matters
Legal, medical, or scientific document retrieval requiring relationship understanding
Research applications evaluating graph-enhanced vs naive RAG
Teams willing to pay extra indexing cost for retrieval quality improvement
avoid_when: Simple Q&A over well-structured documents (naive RAG is cheaper/faster)
Production applications requiring SLA and support
High document ingestion volume (slow + costly indexing)
summary: LightRAG is a graph-enhanced RAG system from HKUDS that builds knowledge graphs from documents alongside vector embeddings, enabling multi-hop reasoning that naive RAG struggles with. Supports naive, local, global, and hybrid retrieval modes. Research-grade software with impressive benchmark results but limited production readiness. Best when retrieval quality for complex relationships is more important than simplicity or cost.
